tagline: Find where you are — and where to go next
intro: Career guidance that goes all the way: an interest assessment, a catalog of careers, and how to actually get in — for teens and adults.
tos_body: Draft. The full terms will be published before public launch.
privacy_body: We collect the minimum. The assessment can be taken anonymously, without an account. Data is deleted on request. The full policy will be published before launch.
doc_updated: Last updated: July 2026. This is a working draft; the final text will be reviewed by a lawyer before public launch.
tos_intro: These are the terms of use for Astrolab (astrolab.nikam.dev), a career-guidance platform. By using the service you accept them. If you are under 18, use the service with the knowledge of a parent or legal guardian.
tos_s1_d: Astrolab helps you get your bearings among professions: you take a test, get an interest profile and matched occupations with study paths. It is an informational and educational service.
tos_s2_d: Results are an estimate based on the RIASEC model, not a diagnosis and not a guarantee of admission, employment or income. You make your own decisions about career and education; always verify exact requirements on official university sites.
tos_s3_d: The service is currently free. If paid features appear, payment terms will be described separately and in advance, with no hidden charges.
tos_s4_d: You can take the test without an account. An account (by email) is only needed to save your results and come back to them. Answer honestly — the usefulness of the result depends on it. Don't create an account on someone else's email.
tos_s5_d: You may not disrupt the service, try to hack it, mass-export data, impersonate another person or use the service for unlawful purposes.
tos_s6_d: The texts, methodology and design belong to the Astrolab project. Your answers belong to you; how we handle them is described in the Privacy Policy.
tos_s7_d: The service is provided "as is". We work to keep it accurate, but we're not responsible for decisions you make based on the results, and it doesn't replace a professional's advice.
tos_s8_d: We may update these terms; we'll flag material changes on the site.
tos_contact_d: Questions about the terms: nick@nikam.dev. Operator: the Astrolab project (nikam.dev). The service is provided as is; nothing here overrides the mandatory consumer rights you have where you live.
privacy_intro: Astrolab serves teenagers among others, so privacy is designed in. The core principle is anonymous by default and minimal data. Operator: the Astrolab project (nikam.dev). We follow privacy-by-design and data minimization: we collect as little as we can and never sell it.
privacy_s1_d: The whole test — questions, profile, result and share link — works with no account and no personal data. A session is keyed by a random token, not by you personally.
privacy_s2_d: Your test answers, age range, country and education stage — to compute the result. If you create an account, only an email (or a Telegram id). We don't ask for your name. Adults may paste a description of their experience or a resume — it is used only for matching and is not stored as a separate document.
privacy_s3_d: Data is used only to show your result and to match professions and study paths. We don't sell data and don't use it for advertising.
privacy_s4_d: Anonymous sessions — up to 90 days, then deleted automatically. Share links — 180 days. Account data — until you delete it. Backups are rotated within 30 days.
privacy_s5_d: Sign-in and share links are stored only as a hash, not in the clear. The connection is encrypted (HTTPS).
privacy_s6_d: If you're under 14, it's better to take the test together with a parent. Creating an account under 18 requires a parent's consent (checked and recorded). A parent never sees your raw answers — only strengths and how to support you.
privacy_s7_d: We use privacy-friendly traffic analytics (GoatCounter): it counts visits without cookies and without personal data, doesn't track you across sites and shows no ads. We use no ad pixels or trackers like Google Analytics. AI processes answers to write explanations; the raw text of your answers doesn't go into logs.
privacy_s8_d: You can delete your data and your account in one click from your account page — it cascades to all sessions, answers and results. If you need help, email nick@nikam.dev.
privacy_contact_d: Delete your data and account yourself from your account page. For other data questions — nick@nikam.dev. Operator: the Astrolab project (nikam.dev).
catalog_intro: How professions really work: what people do, who they suit, and where they lead. Explore what interests you — then check if it fits.
prof_estimate_note: The interest profile is a preliminary estimate based on the RIASEC model, not a diagnosis.
src_bls_tip: US Bureau of Labor Statistics wage data
src_rosstat_tip: Rosstat wage survey, scaled to recent wage growth
src_est_tip: Rough estimate, not from a data source
src_hh_tip: Based on salary analysis of live hh.ru job postings across Russia, refreshed regularly
src_ine_tip: INE (Spain) wage-structure survey microdata by occupation group, scaled to current levels
src_adzuna_tip: Based on salary analysis of live Spanish job postings (Adzuna), refreshed regularly
src_insee_tip: INSEE salary distribution (Structure of Earnings, private sector) by socio-professional group — a group estimate, gross annual
method_data_fr_d: Two sources: salary analysis of live job ads (Adzuna, 25th–75th percentile of real postings) for occupations with enough salaried ads, and INSEE's salary distribution by socio-professional group (Structure of Earnings, private sector) for the rest. All figures are gross annual. Public-sector jobs (police, firefighters) aren't in this private-sector survey.
src_entgeltatlas_tip: Median pay from Germany's Federal Employment Agency (Entgeltatlas) by requirement level, gross annual — a group estimate
method_data_de_d: Two sources: salary analysis of live job ads (Adzuna, 25th–75th percentile of real postings) for occupations with enough salaried ads, and the Federal Employment Agency's median pay (Entgeltatlas) by requirement level for the rest. All figures are gross annual; the median is real, the range is an estimate around it. Very high salaries are capped by the social-security ceiling (Beitragsbemessungsgrenze).
method_data_es_d: Two sources: live job-posting salary analysis (Adzuna, 25th–75th percentile of real offers) for professions with enough salary-tagged ads, and INE's official wage-structure survey microdata (EES, occupation group, full-time, scaled to current levels) for the rest. All figures are gross annual.
edu_disclaimer: Approximate pathways. Requirements and test policies vary by state and school - always verify on the college's or program's official site.
edu_none: No verified data for this country yet. Check requirements on official university and admissions sites.
riasec_caption: Interest profile from the RIASEC model. An estimate, not a diagnosis.
home_chip: Your future is brighter than it looks
home_title: Find work that lights you up
home_lead: 12 minutes and you see professions that fit the real you: what they do, who they suit, and how to get there.
home_how_1_d: 12 minutes, no sign-up. Answer quick cards, there are no right answers.
home_how_2_d: Interests, values and strengths on a clear map, not a dry list.
home_how_3_d: Fitting professions with a real 'why you' and how to actually get there.
home_get_1: Your interest and value profile on a RIASEC map
home_get_2: Three baskets of professions: core, nearby and dark horses
home_get_3: A personal 'why it fits you' for each profession
home_get_4: How to get in: fields of study, exams and deadlines
home_honesty_1: An estimate, not a verdict — your profile changes with experience.
home_honesty_2: Anonymous: no sign-up and no extra data about you.
home_honesty_3: Built on the RIASEC model, not the stars or a social-media quiz.
home_whom_teen: Teenagers — choose what and where to study.
home_whom_adult: Adults — find your thing and get out of a job you don't like.
home_whom_parent: Parents — understand your child and support, not push.
home_final_t: Find out who you can become
home_final_d: Twelve minutes and you have a map, not a fog.
method_lede: Astrolab is an estimate based on the RIASEC model, not fortune-telling and not a medical diagnosis. Here's honestly what's under the hood, and what it does not do.
method_riasec_d: An international standard: six interest types — Realistic, Investigative, Artistic, Social, Enterprising, Conventional. O*NET tags thousands of occupations with it, so your profile maps to real work, not to the stars.
method_klimov_d: Some items also look at the object of work: people, technology, nature, signs, images. It's a classic vocational-psychology lens that complements the RIASEC picture.
method_values_t: Values — a forced choice, not "do you like it"
method_values_d: We don't ask "is this important to you" — almost everyone says yes. Instead you pick between two values each time. That shows what's genuinely a priority for you, not just what sounds good.
method_subjects_d: What you like and what comes easily hints at which paths are realistic, not just desirable.
method_src_klimov: E. A. Klimov's typology of professions
method_det_t: Math does the counting, not «a neural net»
method_det_d: The fit with an occupation is a transparent formula: how close your interest, value and subject profile is to the occupation's profile. It's reproducible: the same answers give the same result.
method_age_d: The interest items are the same for everyone — RIASEC works at any age. What changes is the weighting: teens weight interests and subjects more, adults weight values and experience. Adults also take an «your experience» step, and advice is framed as a transition, not a fresh start.
method_ai_t: What the AI does — and doesn't
method_ai_d: The AI doesn't decide who you should be, and doesn't invent occupations. It only reorders the already-matched options and writes a clear «why it fits you». If the AI is unavailable, the result still works.
method_limit_1: It's an estimate, not a diagnosis or a verdict. Your profile changes with experience — retake it every six months.
method_limit_2: It's an adaptation of the RIASEC model, not a clinical psychological test. We won't say «validated» until we've shown it on our own data.
method_limit_3: Early version of the methodology: we calibrate it as assessments accumulate and keep improving the items.
method_limit_4: The test shows direction and fit, not ability — and certainly not a person's worth.
method_data_t: Where the salary data comes from
method_data_ru_d: The primary source is a salary analysis of live hh.ru job postings across Russia: the range on a profession page is the 25th–75th percentile of real offers (pre-tax). The data is refreshed regularly. For rare professions with few salary-tagged postings we use Rosstat's occupational wage survey, scaled to current levels.
method_data_us_d: US Bureau of Labor Statistics data (the OEWS survey): national annual wages. If there is no reliable data for a profession, we simply don't show a salary.
method_data_badge_d: Every number on a profession page carries a source badge: BLS — or “estimate” when the figure is approximate and not from an official source.
method_s4_d: You can take the test anonymously, without an account. We collect the minimum.
test_intro: Answer quick cards, there are no right answers. At the end we show the professions closest to you.
test_anon_note: No sign-up. You can save the result via a link.
block_a_intro: How much does each activity appeal to you?
block_b_intro: Mark what you like and what comes easily.
block_c_intro: What matters to you in work?
block_c_pairs_intro: Everything has a trade-off. Each time, pick what matters more to you — there are no perfect options here.
val_achievement_desc: Getting results, growing, taking on hard problems
val_autonomy_desc: Deciding for yourself how and when you work
val_recognition_desc: Being noticed and valued for your contribution
val_relationships_desc: Warm people around you and teamwork
val_conditions_desc: Comfort, balance and a convenient schedule
cv_intro: Paste your resume or briefly describe what you've done. The 'why you' notes will then account for your background and frame a transition, not a fresh start.
cv_placeholder: For example: 6 years in B2B sales, led a team of five, lots of negotiation and CRM work. I'd like to move closer to analytics…
cv_note: Optional. The text is used only for matching and is not stored as a resume.
result_values: What matters to you at work
result_dark_sub: Unexpected options to widen your view
result_disclaimer: An estimate, not a verdict. Your profile changes with experience, retake it every six months.
next_save_hint: Sign in to save this plan and check off steps
parent_intro: Your child took a career-orientation test and chose to share the result with you. This is the gist only — strengths and how to help — with no raw answers and no scores. It's already great that they're thinking about the future.
parent_footer: This is a compass, not a verdict — interests still shift at this age. The best thing you can do is help them try things.
interview_intro: A few questions about you, to make the explanations sharper and more personal.
result_empty: Professions are coming soon to the catalog.
login_intro: Enter your email — we'll send a sign-in link. No password.
login_sent_d: If we have that address, a sign-in link is on its way. It's valid for 30 minutes.
login_note: An account is only for saving your results and coming back to them. You can take the test without one.
verify_fail: The link is invalid or expired. Try signing in again.
me_empty: No saved results yet. Take the test and save it to your account.
me_delete_confirm: Permanently delete your account and all results? This can't be undone.
result_save_login: Sign in and save the result
result_consent: I'm 18+, or my parent doesn't mind saving the result
